name: azure-resource-manager-postgresql-dotnet
description: Azure Resource Manager SDK reference for managing PostgreSQL Flexible Server deployments in .NET. Provides code examples for creating servers, databases, firewall rules, configurations, backups, and high availability setups.
capabilities: Create and configure PostgreSQL Flexible Server instances with custom SKUs, storage, and high availability
Manage databases, firewall rules, and server configurations via C# code
Set up Entra ID authentication for Azure PostgreSQL
Configure backups and perform point-in-time restores
Create and manage read replicas for read-heavy workloads
Scale server resources up or down dynamically
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. The static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected no suspicious patterns. The skill provides Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server SDK reference documentation with code examples. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: This skill provides code examples for managing Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server using the .NET SDK. It helps developers automate database provisioning, configuration, backups, and high availability without manually navigating the Azure portal.
limitations: Does not execute actual Azure operations - provides reference code only
Does not include connection pooling or query optimization guidance
Does not provide real-time monitoring or alerting configuration
Requires user to have Azure subscription and appropriate permissions

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers building Azure infrastructure automation
useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Automating Deployments
useCases.description: Automate PostgreSQL server provisioning as part of CI/CD pipelines. Create infrastructure-as-code scripts that spin up dev/staging/prod environments with consistent configurations.

useCases.title: Developer Managing Test Databases
useCases.description: Quickly create and tear down test databases for automated testing. Use code examples to set up isolated test environments with proper firewall rules.

useCases.targetUser: Platform engineers creating internal database management tools
useCases.title: Platform Engineer Building Internal Tools
useCases.description: Build internal tooling for team members to manage PostgreSQL resources without Azure portal access. Create custom dashboards or self-service portals.

promptTemplates.title: Create PostgreSQL Server
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server using the .NET SDK with high availability enabled.

promptTemplates.title: Configure Firewall Rules
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I add a firewall rule to allow an IP range to connect to my PostgreSQL Flexible Server?

promptTemplates.title: Set Up Entra ID Admin
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure Entra ID (Azure AD) authentication for my PostgreSQL Flexible Server using C#.

promptTemplates.title: Backup and Restore
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to list backups and perform a point-in-time restore on Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential instead of hardcoded connection strings for authentication
Enable zone-redundant high availability for production workloads
Configure both Entra ID and password authentication for flexibility during migration
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode admin passwords in source code - use Azure Key Vault or environment variables
Avoid using public network access for production servers - prefer private endpoints
Do not skip backup configuration - ensure adequate retention for compliance
Avoid creating servers in deprecated Single Server tier - use Flexible Server

faq.question: What is the difference between Flexible Server and Single Server?
faq.answer: Flexible Server is the modern, recommended deployment option with more features, better scaling, and zone-redundant HA. Single Server is deprecated and scheduled for retirement.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure PostgreSQL from my .NET application?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity, or configure service principal authentication. For Entra ID, acquire tokens using Azure Identity library.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to manage existing servers?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes examples for getting existing servers, listing resources, updating configurations, and managing firewall rules.

faq.question: What PostgreSQL versions are supported?
faq.answer: The SDK supports PostgreSQL 11 through 16. Use the PostgreSqlFlexibleServerVersion enum to specify the version.

faq.question: How do I handle connection errors in my code?
faq.answer: Use try-catch with RequestFailedException to handle Azure-specific errors. Check ex.Status for error codes like 409 (conflict) or 400 (bad request).

faq.question: Can I create read replicas with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use CreateMode.Replica with SourceServerResourceId to create read replicas for scaling read-heavy workloads.
